name: sf-install
description: Binary-assertion test suite for /sf:install. Covers fresh-machine flow, partial-Stage-2 resume, joiner detection, and the three team-lead pushbacks (always-check Stage 1, additive-diff Stage 5, manual handoff).

tests.expected_output_summary: All 7 stages complete; doctor green; identity + feed populated; walkthrough acknowledged.
binary_assertions: After the run, $XDG_STATE_HOME/sf/install-state.json exists and validates against install-state.schema.json
completed_stages contains all of [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]
stage_artifacts.2.plugins_installed has exactly 6 entries matching the pinned-version registry
stage_artifacts.3.feed_state is either 'first-friend-bootstrap' or 'joiner-clone' (never 'inaccessible' on a passing fixture)
stage_artifacts.4.identity_path points at ~/.startup-framework/wiki/identity.md and that file exists
stage_artifacts.5.wiki_root points at an existing wiki with index.md, log.md, identity.md, LICENSES.md present
stage_artifacts.6.doctor_passed is true
stage_artifacts.7.walkthrough_acknowledged is true
No file under ~/.startup-framework/wiki/ contains any forbidden substring from wiki-skeleton/tests/forbidden-substrings.txt

tests.expected_output_summary: Checkpoint shows 3 of 6 plugins installed; resume picks up at plugin 4 without reinstalling 1-3.
binary_assertions: Pre-run checkpoint state.completed_stages does NOT contain 2
Pre-run state.stage_artifacts.2.plugins_installed has 3 entries (context-mode, claude-mem, superpowers)
Skill output indicates it is resuming at Stage 2 plugin index 4 (skill-creator)
No /plugin install command is issued for context-mode, claude-mem, or superpowers during the run
After successful run, stage_artifacts.2.plugins_installed has all 6 entries
After successful run, completed_stages contains 2 (and 3..7)

tests.expected_output_summary: Stage 3 detects existing feed; skips bootstrap; clones; surfaces other friends' handles. Stage 5 still creates a fresh wiki (no group history inheritance per ADR-017).
binary_assertions: Stage 3 calls feed.detect_repo_state exactly once
feed.detect_repo_state returns mode='joiner-clone'
Stage 3 calls feed.clone_existing (NOT feed.bootstrap_first_friend)
Stage 3 surfaces existing_handles from the detection result to the friend
Stage 5 creates a fresh wiki skeleton (the friend's local wiki/ did not pre-exist)
No file under the friend's wiki contains content copied from any other friend's wiki
Stage 3's mini-handle prompt rejects any handle that appears in existing_handles

tests.expected_output_summary: Even with a green Stage 1 in the checkpoint, the probes run again on re-invocation.
binary_assertions: First run completes Stage 1, marks it in completed_stages
Second run (same command, no env change): Stage 1 still invokes claude auth status, gh auth status, node --version, git --version, and env-var lookups
Second run does NOT prompt the friend for remediation (everything was already green)
If between runs the friend's gh auth state changes from logged-in to logged-out, the second run DOES surface the failure (proving the check is not stale-cached)

tests.expected_output_summary: Re-running install against an existing wiki triggers Stage 5 additive-diff mode and never overwrites.
binary_assertions: First run creates ~/.startup-framework/wiki/ from the skeleton
Friend edits ~/.startup-framework/wiki/index.md by hand (adds a section)
Second run: Stage 5 detects existing files; switches to additive-diff mode; lists 'would_write' for any new templates and 'already_present' for existing files
Second run: if friend declines the diff, NO existing files are touched and no new files are written
Second run: if friend accepts, only the 'would_write' entries are written; existing files (including the hand-edited index.md) are byte-identical to their pre-run state
Under no path does Stage 5 overwrite an existing file regardless of flags or modes

tests.expected_output_summary: After install completes, no follow-up slash command is auto-invoked. Friend triggers the next action.
binary_assertions: After Stage 7 acknowledgment, the orchestrator exits cleanly without calling /sf:wake-up, /sf:doctor, /sf:bootstrap-project, or any other slash command
The Stage 7 walkthrough suggests next actions (e.g. /sf:wake-up) but does NOT execute them
The plugin's install hook (if any) does not invoke /sf:install at PostInstall time — friend must type the three-command sequence per ADR-019

tests.expected_output_summary: Stage 3 feed.clone_existing fails (simulated 403); install aborts cleanly; re-run resumes at Stage 3.
binary_assertions: Mid-run, feed.clone_existing raises a recoverable error (simulated 403)
Orchestrator appends an entry to state.abort_log with stage=3 and a short error_summary
completed_stages does NOT include 3
Skill output to the friend includes 're-run /sf:install to resume from Stage 3'
Exit code is non-zero
Re-run after the simulated 403 is cleared: install resumes at Stage 3, does NOT re-execute Stages 1-2's plugin installs
Re-run completes all 7 stages and final checkpoint shows all stages done

tests.expected_output_summary: Friend confirms; checkpoint deleted; wiki + plugins + identity left untouched.
binary_assertions: Skill prompts for confirmation before deleting checkpoint
If confirmed, $XDG_STATE_HOME/sf/install-state.json is deleted
No file under ~/.startup-framework/wiki/ is modified or deleted
No plugin is uninstalled
Activity Feed clone at ~/.startup-framework/activity-feed/ is unchanged
Subsequent /sf:install (without --reset) starts from Stage 1 as if fresh

notes: Pushback tests P1/P2/P3 are explicit because team-lead called them out by name. They're load-bearing for the install contract.
Fixtures simulate environment + filesystem state + feed responses. The eval harness substitutes the simulated feed for real GitHub I/O.
Trigger-test true on fresh-machine-complete only — the other tests exercise downstream behavior and shouldn't double-count toward Layer 1 activation.
